summary: A compact two-story stone brick tower with a gable roof made of dark wood. The structure features a symmetrical square footprint with regularly spaced windows on both floors and a foundation of gravel. The building sits on a raised dirt platform surrounded by grass.
elements: windows (4 per floor, arranged in grid)
door (visible on one side, appears to be dark wood)
rebuild_hints: Build 8x8 base of stonebrick, 2 blocks high for main walls
Add gravel layer beneath and around foundation for platform effect
Place 4 windows per floor in symmetrical pattern (2x2 grid per side)
Construct gable roof using dark wood planks, stepping inward toward peak
Add dark wood trim or frame around windows
Include door on one side at ground level with dark wood frame
Ensure all four sides are identical for rotational symmetry
uncertainties: Exact window size and frame material (appears to be glass with stone trim)
Interior layout and floor height spacing
Precise roof peak height and slope angle
Whether foundation extends fully under building or is decorative
